user.title: System Topology
user.description: Select the grid topology before providing entity bindings.

data_description.phase_mode: Choose between single-phase or three-phase operation.

battery_strategy.name: Battery SOC Targets
battery_strategy.description: These values define how much charge the planner tries to keep in the battery under normal and emergency conditions.

data_description.min_soc_threshold: Minimum battery charge level to maintain (%)
data_description.arbitrage_mode_reserve_soc: Minimum reserve SOC kept in the battery during arbitrage mode (%)
data_description.arbitrage_mode_deadline_hour: Local hour of the next arbitrage cutoff (shared by arbitrage selling and Negative Arbitrage Buy). Before it the planner targets today; after it, tomorrow
data_description.negative_buy_threshold: Net buy price (€/kWh) at or below which Negative Arbitrage Buy mode treats a slot as paid-to-consume. Default: -0.05
data_description.emergency_soc_threshold: SOC level below which emergency charging is triggered regardless of price (%)
data_description.soc_buffer_target: SOC level above which no price relaxation is applied. Below this, the price threshold is gradually relaxed (%)
data_description.soc_price_multiplier_max: Maximum price threshold multiplier when battery is at/below emergency SOC (1.3 = accept 30% higher prices)

forecast_entities.name: Solar Forecast Behavior
forecast_entities.description: These settings decide when the planner trusts solar forecasts and how much battery headroom it keeps for sunny days.

data_description.solar_forecast_start_hour: Hour of the day (12-23) when the system starts reading tomorrow's solar forecast. Earlier = more time to react, later = more accurate forecast. Default: 20 (8 PM)
data_description.max_soc_threshold_sunny: Max SOC for grid charging when tomorrow is sunny (%). Grid charging stops at this level to leave room for free solar energy
data_description.max_soc_threshold_solar: Battery SOC above which solar surplus is diverted to the EV / export instead of being absorbed by the battery (%). Independent from grid charging ceiling.
data_description.sunny_forecast_threshold_kwh: Solar forecast threshold in kWh that activates sunny-day grid charging mode
data_description.significant_solar_threshold: Solar surplus power level considered significant enough to prefer over grid (W)

price_strategy.name: Price Logic
price_strategy.description: These thresholds control when the planner considers buying from the grid cheap enough or exporting solar valuable enough.

data_description.price_adjustment_multiplier: Multiplier applied to raw price (e.g. 1.04 for the Energie.be dynamic contract). Default: 1.04
data_description.price_adjustment_offset: Fixed €/kWh offset added after multiplier (e.g. 0.005 for 0.5 c€ on an excl. VAT market basis). Default: 0.005
data_description.very_low_price_threshold: Percentage of daily price range considered 'very low' (e.g., 30 = bottom 30%)
data_description.feedin_price_threshold: Price threshold above which solar export to grid is enabled (€/kWh)
data_description.feedin_adjustment_multiplier: Multiplier applied to raw price when calculating net feed-in value. Default: 1.0
data_description.feedin_adjustment_offset: Fixed €/kWh offset added to feed-in price (negative values model costs). Default: -0.0098
data_description.use_dynamic_threshold: Enable intelligent price analysis for more selective charging at better prices
data_description.dynamic_threshold_confidence: Confidence required for dynamic charging decisions (higher = more selective)
data_description.use_average_threshold: Use 24-hour rolling average of future prices as the charging threshold

car_strategy.name: Car Charging Rules
car_strategy.description: These values only affect EV charging behavior and how permissive the planner can be when deciding to start.

data_description.min_car_charging_duration: Minimum consecutive hours of low prices required before starting car charging
data_description.car_permissive_threshold_multiplier: Multiplier applied to car charging threshold when permissive mode is active (1.2 = +20% higher threshold)
data_description.car_use_battery_arbitrage: When arbitrage export is active, let the EV consume that power locally before the remainder is exported to grid

inverter_strategy.name: Inverter Export Control
inverter_strategy.description: These settings tune how strongly the planner derates solar export when feed-in should be limited.

data_description.max_inverter_power: Maximum inverter output used when feed-in is allowed and as the upper cap for derating guidance (W)
data_description.inverter_export_limit: Grid export target used while feed-in is blocked. The planner aims to keep net export around this value, for example 80W (W)
data_description.inverter_export_deadband: Allowed deviation around the export target before the planner changes derating. With the default 40W deadband and 80W target, the planner holds steady between 40W and 120W export (W)
data_description.inverter_derating_unused_release_minutes: Minutes to wait before raising the inverter cap by one small step when export stays safely below the target. Higher values make derating reopen more cautiously. Default: 5 minutes
data_description.inverter_derating_soc_bypass_threshold: If average battery SOC is below this threshold, the planner stops derating and lets the inverter run freely so solar can charge the battery (%)

data_description.min_soc_threshold: Minimum battery charge level to maintain (%)
data_description.arbitrage_mode_reserve_soc: Minimum reserve SOC kept in the battery during arbitrage mode (%)
data_description.arbitrage_mode_deadline_hour: Local hour of the next arbitrage cutoff (shared by arbitrage selling and Negative Arbitrage Buy). Before it the planner targets today; after it, tomorrow
data_description.negative_buy_threshold: Net buy price (€/kWh) at or below which Negative Arbitrage Buy mode treats a slot as paid-to-consume. Default: -0.05
data_description.solar_forecast_start_hour: Hour of the day (12-23) when the system starts reading tomorrow's solar forecast. Earlier = more time to react, later = more accurate forecast. Default: 20 (8 PM)
data_description.max_soc_threshold_sunny: Max SOC for grid charging when tomorrow is sunny (%). Grid charging stops at this level to leave room for free solar energy
data_description.max_soc_threshold_solar: Battery SOC above which solar surplus is diverted to the EV / export instead of being absorbed by the battery (%). Independent from grid charging ceiling.
data_description.sunny_forecast_threshold_kwh: Solar forecast threshold in kWh that activates sunny-day grid charging mode
data_description.price_adjustment_multiplier: Multiplier applied to raw price (e.g. 1.04 for the Energie.be dynamic contract). Default: 1.04
data_description.price_adjustment_offset: Fixed €/kWh offset added after multiplier (e.g. 0.005 for 0.5 c€ on an excl. VAT market basis). Default: 0.005
data_description.emergency_soc_threshold: SOC level below which emergency charging is triggered regardless of price (%)
data_description.soc_buffer_target: SOC level above which no price relaxation is applied. Below this, the price threshold is gradually relaxed (%)
data_description.soc_price_multiplier_max: Maximum price threshold multiplier when battery is at/below emergency SOC (1.3 = accept 30% higher prices)
data_description.very_low_price_threshold: Percentage of daily price range considered 'very low' (e.g., 30 = bottom 30%)
data_description.significant_solar_threshold: Solar surplus power level considered significant enough to prefer over grid (W)
data_description.feedin_price_threshold: Price threshold above which solar export to grid is enabled (€/kWh)
data_description.feedin_adjustment_multiplier: Multiplier applied to raw price when calculating net feed-in value. Default: 1.0
data_description.feedin_adjustment_offset: Fixed €/kWh offset added to feed-in price (negative values model costs). Default: -0.0098
data_description.use_dynamic_threshold: Enable intelligent price analysis for more selective charging at better prices
data_description.dynamic_threshold_confidence: Confidence required for dynamic charging decisions (higher = more selective)
data_description.use_average_threshold: Use 24-hour rolling average of future prices as the charging threshold
data_description.min_car_charging_duration: Minimum consecutive hours of low prices required before starting car charging
data_description.car_permissive_threshold_multiplier: Multiplier applied to car charging threshold when permissive mode is active (1.2 = +20% higher threshold)
data_description.car_use_battery_arbitrage: When arbitrage export is active, let the EV consume that power locally before the remainder is exported to grid
data_description.max_inverter_power: Maximum inverter output used when feed-in is allowed and as the upper cap for derating guidance (W)
data_description.inverter_export_limit: Grid export target used while feed-in is blocked. The planner aims to keep net export around this value, for example 80W (W)
data_description.inverter_export_deadband: Allowed deviation around the export target before the planner changes derating. With the default 40W deadband and 80W target, the planner holds steady between 40W and 120W export (W)
data_description.inverter_derating_unused_release_minutes: Minutes to wait before raising the inverter cap by one small step when export stays safely below the target. Higher values make derating reopen more cautiously. Default: 5 minutes
data_description.inverter_derating_soc_bypass_threshold: If average battery SOC is below this threshold, the planner stops derating and lets the inverter run freely so solar can charge the battery (%)

entities.title: Entity Selection
entities.description: Select sensors and entities used by the planner.

price_entities.name: Prices & Batteries
price_entities.description: Core inputs the planner needs for price comparison and battery state tracking.

data_description.nordpool_config_entry: Select the Nord Pool config entry to enable full interval price data
data_description.current_price_entity: Current electricity price from Nord Pool integration
data_description.highest_price_entity: Highest electricity price today from Nord Pool
data_description.lowest_price_entity: Lowest electricity price today from Nord Pool
data_description.next_price_entity: Next hour electricity price from Nord Pool
data_description.battery_soc_entities: Entities providing battery state of charge (%)

single_phase_entities.name: Single-Phase Power Sensors
single_phase_entities.description: Use these sensors when your installation is monitored as one combined phase.

data_description.house_consumption_entity: Current house power consumption in W

three_phase_entities.name: Three-Phase Power Sensors
three_phase_entities.description: Optional per-phase sensors for L1, L2 and L3. Leave any field empty if that measurement is unavailable.

data_description.phase_1_solar_entity: Phase L1 solar production sensor (leave blank if unavailable)
data_description.phase_1_consumption_entity: Phase L1 house consumption sensor (leave blank if unavailable)
data_description.phase_1_car_entity: Phase L1 EV charger power sensor (leave blank if unavailable)
data_description.phase_1_battery_power_entity: Phase L1 battery power sensor in W (negative = charging, leave blank if unavailable)
data_description.phase_2_solar_entity: Phase L2 solar production sensor (leave blank if unavailable)
data_description.phase_2_consumption_entity: Phase L2 house consumption sensor (leave blank if unavailable)
data_description.phase_2_car_entity: Phase L2 EV charger power sensor (leave blank if unavailable)
data_description.phase_2_battery_power_entity: Phase L2 battery power sensor in W (negative = charging, leave blank if unavailable)
data_description.phase_3_solar_entity: Phase L3 solar production sensor (leave blank if unavailable)
data_description.phase_3_consumption_entity: Phase L3 house consumption sensor (leave blank if unavailable)
data_description.phase_3_car_entity: Phase L3 EV charger power sensor (leave blank if unavailable)
data_description.phase_3_battery_power_entity: Phase L3 battery power sensor in W (negative = charging, leave blank if unavailable)

grid_costs.name: Grid & Tariffs
grid_costs.description: Optional network cost inputs used to calculate a more realistic buy price and power limits.

data_description.monthly_grid_peak_entity: Current month grid peak in W for power management
data_description.p1_tariff_entity: P1 meter tariff sensor (1=day, 2=night) for day/night transport cost switching
data_description.transport_cost_day: Fluvius network tariff for day hours (€/kWh, excl. VAT)
data_description.transport_cost_night: Fluvius network tariff for night and weekends (€/kWh, excl. VAT)
data_description.grid_power_entity: Real-time grid power in W (positive = import, negative = export) used for peak detection and inverter derating diagnostics

forecast_entities.name: Solar Forecast
forecast_entities.description: Forecast sensors are optional, but they improve overnight charging decisions by keeping room for tomorrow's solar.

data_description.solar_forecast_entity: Tomorrow's solar production forecast in kWh (e.g. sensor.energy_production_tomorrow). Read from the forecast start hour onwards to decide overnight grid charging
data_description.solar_forecast_today_entity: Today's solar production forecast in kWh (e.g. sensor.energy_production_today). Used between midnight and the forecast start hour to validate the cached forecast

data_description.current_price_entity: Current electricity price from Nord Pool integration
data_description.highest_price_entity: Highest electricity price today from Nord Pool
data_description.lowest_price_entity: Lowest electricity price today from Nord Pool
data_description.next_price_entity: Next hour electricity price from Nord Pool

data_description.transport_cost_night: Fluvius network tariff for night and weekends (€/kWh, excl. VAT)

battery_capacities.title: Battery Capacities
battery_capacities.description: Provide nominal capacity for each battery sensor (kWh).

battery_phase_assignment.title: Battery Phase Assignment
battery_phase_assignment.description: Map each battery to the phases it can charge or discharge.

safety_limits.title: Safety Limits
safety_limits.description: Configure power and SOC safety limits. {validation_errors}

power_limits.name: Power Limits
power_limits.description: Hard limits that the planner should never exceed while charging from the grid.

data_description.max_battery_power: Maximum power limit for battery charging/discharging (W)
data_description.max_car_power: Maximum power limit for car charging (W)
data_description.max_grid_power: Maximum power limit from grid — safety limit (W)
data_description.arbitrage_mode_max_export_power: Maximum export power used during arbitrage mode. Set to 0 to use the automatic cap from the battery and grid limits (W)
data_description.base_grid_setpoint: Base minimum grid setpoint when no monthly peak data available (W)

predictive_controls.name: Charge Detection
predictive_controls.description: Inputs that help the planner detect when a car is really charging and when predictive charging should become active.

data_description.min_car_charging_threshold: Minimum power to consider car as actively charging (W)
data_description.predictive_charging_min_soc: Minimum SOC for predictive charging logic (%)

data_description.max_battery_power: Maximum power limit for battery charging/discharging (W)
data_description.max_car_power: Maximum power limit for car charging (W)
data_description.max_grid_power: Maximum power limit from grid — safety limit (W)
data_description.arbitrage_mode_max_export_power: Maximum export power used during arbitrage mode. Set to 0 to use the automatic cap from the battery and grid limits (W)
data_description.min_car_charging_threshold: Minimum power to consider car as actively charging (W)
data_description.predictive_charging_min_soc: Minimum SOC for predictive charging logic (%)
data_description.base_grid_setpoint: Base minimum grid setpoint when no monthly peak data available (W)

set_manual_override.name: Set Manual Override
set_manual_override.description: Set battery/car charging decision overrides or numeric charger/grid overrides.

entry_id.name: Config Entry ID
entry_id.description: Optional config entry ID of the Electricity Planner instance (required when multiple instances are configured).

target.name: Target
target.description: Which manual override target to set: battery, car, both, charger_limit, or grid_setpoint.

action.name: Action
action.description: Force charge (enable charging) or force wait (disable charging) for battery, car, or both targets.

duration.name: Duration
duration.description: Manual override duration in minutes (1-1440). Optional - omit for indefinite override.

reason.name: Reason
reason.description: Optional reason for the override (for logging and diagnostics).

charger_limit.name: Charger Limit
charger_limit.description: Manual EV charger limit in watts. Use with target charger_limit or bundle with a car override.

grid_setpoint.name: Grid Setpoint
grid_setpoint.description: Manual grid import/export setpoint in watts. Use with target grid_setpoint or bundle with a car override.

clear_manual_override.name: Clear Manual Override
clear_manual_override.description: Remove active manual overrides and return battery/car/charger/grid control to automatic decision making.

entry_id.name: Config Entry ID
entry_id.description: Optional config entry ID of the Electricity Planner instance (required when multiple instances are configured).

target.name: Target
target.description: Which manual override target to clear. all clears battery/car/charger_limit/grid_setpoint.

init.title: System Topology
init.description: Review and update the planner configuration step by step.

topology_section.name: Topology
topology_section.description: Switch between single-phase and three-phase mode here. Existing single-phase bindings are kept so you can migrate gradually.

data_description.phase_mode: Choose between single-phase or three-phase operation.

price_entities.description: Core price sensors and battery SOC inputs used by the planner.

single_phase_entities.name: Single-Phase Power Sensors
single_phase_entities.description: Combined power sensors used for single-phase installations. These can stay configured even if you switch to three-phase mode.

data_description.house_consumption_entity: Current house power consumption in W

grid_costs.name: Grid & Tariffs
grid_costs.description: Optional cost and grid sensors that make buy-price calculations and peak handling more accurate.

data_description.monthly_grid_peak_entity: Current month grid peak in W for power management
data_description.p1_tariff_entity: P1 meter tariff sensor (1=day, 2=night) for day/night transport cost switching
data_description.transport_cost_day: Fluvius network tariff for day hours (€/kWh, excl. VAT)
data_description.transport_cost_night: Fluvius network tariff for night and weekends (€/kWh, excl. VAT)
data_description.grid_power_entity: Real-time grid power in W (positive = import, negative = export) used for peak detection and inverter derating diagnostics

forecast_entities.name: Forecast & Sunny-Day Rules
forecast_entities.description: Forecast sensors and thresholds that keep room in the battery when strong solar production is expected.

data_description.solar_forecast_entity: Tomorrow's solar production forecast in kWh (e.g. sensor.energy_production_tomorrow). Read from the forecast start hour onwards to decide overnight grid charging
data_description.solar_forecast_today_entity: Today's solar production forecast in kWh (e.g. sensor.energy_production_today). Used between midnight and the forecast start hour to validate the cached forecast
data_description.solar_forecast_start_hour: Hour of the day (12-23) when the system starts reading tomorrow's solar forecast. Earlier = more time to react, later = more accurate forecast. Default: 20 (8 PM)
data_description.max_soc_threshold_sunny: Max SOC for grid charging when tomorrow is sunny (%). Grid charging stops at this level to leave room for free solar energy
data_description.max_soc_threshold_solar: Battery SOC above which solar surplus is diverted to the EV / export instead of being absorbed by the battery (%). Independent from grid charging ceiling.
data_description.sunny_forecast_threshold_kwh: Solar forecast threshold in kWh that activates sunny-day grid charging mode

battery_strategy.name: Battery SOC Targets
battery_strategy.description: Targets that determine how aggressively the planner protects or fills the battery.

data_description.min_soc_threshold: Minimum battery charge level to maintain (%)
data_description.emergency_soc_threshold: SOC level below which emergency charging is triggered regardless of price (%)
data_description.soc_buffer_target: SOC level above which no price relaxation is applied. Below this, the price threshold is gradually relaxed (%)
data_description.soc_price_multiplier_max: Maximum price threshold multiplier when battery is at/below emergency SOC (1.3 = accept 30% higher prices)

price_strategy.name: Price Logic
price_strategy.description: Thresholds and adjustments for buy and sell price calculations.

data_description.price_adjustment_multiplier: Multiplier applied to raw price (e.g. 1.04 for the Energie.be dynamic contract). Default: 1.04
data_description.price_adjustment_offset: Fixed €/kWh offset added after multiplier (e.g. 0.005 for 0.5 c€ on an excl. VAT market basis). Default: 0.005
data_description.very_low_price_threshold: Percentage of daily price range considered 'very low' (e.g., 30 = bottom 30%)
data_description.significant_solar_threshold: Solar surplus power level considered significant enough to prefer over grid (W)
data_description.feedin_price_threshold: Price threshold above which solar export to grid is enabled (€/kWh)
data_description.feedin_adjustment_multiplier: Multiplier applied to raw price when calculating net feed-in value. Default: 1.0
data_description.feedin_adjustment_offset: Fixed €/kWh offset added to feed-in price (negative values model costs). Default: -0.0098
data_description.use_dynamic_threshold: Enable intelligent price analysis for more selective charging at better prices
data_description.dynamic_threshold_confidence: Confidence required for dynamic charging decisions (higher = more selective)
data_description.use_average_threshold: Use 24-hour rolling average of future prices as the charging threshold

car_strategy.name: Car Charging Rules
car_strategy.description: Settings that affect EV charging windows and permissive mode behavior.

data_description.min_car_charging_duration: Minimum consecutive hours of low prices required before starting car charging
data_description.car_permissive_threshold_multiplier: Multiplier applied to car charging threshold when permissive mode is active (1.2 = +20% higher threshold)
data_description.car_use_battery_arbitrage: When arbitrage export is active, let the EV consume that power locally before the remainder is exported to grid

power_limits.name: Power Limits
power_limits.description: Absolute grid, battery and car limits used as safety boundaries.

data_description.max_battery_power: Maximum power limit for battery charging/discharging (W)
data_description.max_car_power: Maximum power limit for car charging (W)
data_description.max_grid_power: Maximum power limit from grid — safety limit (W)
data_description.min_car_charging_threshold: Minimum power to consider car as actively charging (W)
data_description.predictive_charging_min_soc: Minimum SOC for predictive charging logic (%)
data_description.base_grid_setpoint: Base minimum grid setpoint when no monthly peak data available (W)

inverter_strategy.name: Inverter Export Control
inverter_strategy.description: Advanced controls for keeping feed-in near a target export level.

data_description.max_inverter_power: Maximum inverter output used when feed-in is allowed and as the upper cap for derating guidance (W)
data_description.inverter_export_limit: Grid export target used while feed-in is blocked. The planner aims to keep net export around this value, for example 80W (W)
data_description.inverter_export_deadband: Allowed deviation around the export target before the planner changes derating. With the default 40W deadband and 80W target, the planner holds steady between 40W and 120W export (W)
data_description.inverter_derating_unused_release_minutes: Minutes to wait before raising the inverter cap by one small step when export stays safely below the target. Higher values make derating reopen more cautiously. Default: 5 minutes
data_description.inverter_derating_soc_bypass_threshold: If average battery SOC is below this threshold, the planner stops derating and lets the inverter run freely so solar can charge the battery (%)

battery_capacity_section.name: Battery Capacities
battery_capacity_section.description: Set the nominal capacity for each configured battery sensor in kWh.

battery_phase_mapping.name: Battery Phase Mapping
battery_phase_mapping.description: For three-phase systems, select which phases each battery can charge or discharge on.

three_phase_entities.name: Three-Phase Power Sensors
three_phase_entities.description: Optional per-phase solar, consumption, car and battery power sensors.

data_description.phase_1_solar_entity: Phase L1 solar production sensor (leave blank if unavailable)

data_description.phase_2_car_entity: Phase L2 EV charger power sensor (leave blank if unavailable)
data_description.phase_2_battery_power_entity: Phase L2 battery power sensor in W (negative = charging, leave blank if unavailable)
data_description.phase_3_solar_entity: Phase L3 solar production sensor (leave blank if unavailable)
data_description.phase_3_consumption_entity: Phase L3 house consumption sensor (leave blank if unavailable)
data_description.phase_3_car_entity: Phase L3 EV charger power sensor (leave blank if unavailable)
data_description.phase_3_battery_power_entity: Phase L3 battery power sensor in W (negative = charging, leave blank if unavailable)

battery_capacities.title: Battery Capacities
battery_capacities.description: Update nominal capacity for each battery sensor.

battery_phase_assignment.title: Battery Phase Assignment
battery_phase_assignment.description: Assign batteries to the phases that can charge or discharge them.

data_description.min_soc_threshold: Minimum battery charge level to maintain (%)
data_description.arbitrage_mode_reserve_soc: Minimum reserve SOC kept in the battery during arbitrage mode (%)
data_description.arbitrage_mode_deadline_hour: Local hour of the next arbitrage cutoff (shared by arbitrage selling and Negative Arbitrage Buy). Before it the planner targets today; after it, tomorrow
data_description.negative_buy_threshold: Net buy price (€/kWh) at or below which Negative Arbitrage Buy mode treats a slot as paid-to-consume. Default: -0.05
data_description.solar_forecast_start_hour: Hour of the day (12-23) when the system starts reading tomorrow's solar forecast. Earlier = more time to react, later = more accurate forecast. Default: 20 (8 PM)
data_description.max_soc_threshold_sunny: Max SOC for grid charging when tomorrow is sunny (%). Grid charging stops at this level to leave room for free solar energy
data_description.max_soc_threshold_solar: Battery SOC above which solar surplus is diverted to the EV / export instead of being absorbed by the battery (%). Independent from grid charging ceiling.
data_description.sunny_forecast_threshold_kwh: Solar forecast threshold in kWh that activates sunny-day grid charging mode
data_description.price_adjustment_multiplier: Multiplier applied to raw price (e.g. 1.04 for the Energie.be dynamic contract). Default: 1.04
data_description.price_adjustment_offset: Fixed €/kWh offset added after multiplier (e.g. 0.005 for 0.5 c€ on an excl. VAT market basis). Default: 0.005
data_description.emergency_soc_threshold: SOC level below which emergency charging is triggered regardless of price (%)
data_description.soc_buffer_target: SOC level above which no price relaxation is applied. Below this, the price threshold is gradually relaxed (%)
data_description.soc_price_multiplier_max: Maximum price threshold multiplier when battery is at/below emergency SOC (1.3 = accept 30% higher prices)
data_description.very_low_price_threshold: Percentage of daily price range considered 'very low' (e.g., 30 = bottom 30%)
data_description.significant_solar_threshold: Solar surplus power level considered significant enough to prefer over grid (W)
data_description.feedin_price_threshold: Price threshold above which solar export to grid is enabled (€/kWh)
data_description.feedin_adjustment_multiplier: Multiplier applied to raw price when calculating net feed-in value. Default: 1.0
data_description.feedin_adjustment_offset: Fixed €/kWh offset added to feed-in price (negative values model costs). Default: -0.0098
data_description.use_dynamic_threshold: Enable intelligent price analysis for more selective charging at better prices
data_description.dynamic_threshold_confidence: Confidence required for dynamic charging decisions (higher = more selective)
data_description.use_average_threshold: Use 24-hour rolling average of future prices as the charging threshold
data_description.min_car_charging_duration: Minimum consecutive hours of low prices required before starting car charging
data_description.car_permissive_threshold_multiplier: Multiplier applied to car charging threshold when permissive mode is active (1.2 = +20% higher threshold)
data_description.car_use_battery_arbitrage: When arbitrage export is active, let the EV consume that power locally before the remainder is exported to grid
data_description.max_inverter_power: Maximum inverter output used when feed-in is allowed and as the upper cap for derating guidance (W)
data_description.inverter_export_limit: Grid export target used while feed-in is blocked. The planner aims to keep net export around this value, for example 80W (W)
data_description.inverter_export_deadband: Allowed deviation around the export target before the planner changes derating. With the default 40W deadband and 80W target, the planner holds steady between 40W and 120W export (W)
data_description.inverter_derating_unused_release_minutes: Minutes to wait before raising the inverter cap by one small step when export stays safely below the target. Higher values make derating reopen more cautiously. Default: 5 minutes
data_description.inverter_derating_soc_bypass_threshold: If average battery SOC is below this threshold, the planner stops derating and lets the inverter run freely so solar can charge the battery (%)

safety_limits.title: Safety Limits
safety_limits.description: Configure power and SOC safety limits. {validation_errors}

data_description.max_battery_power: Maximum power limit for battery charging/discharging (W)
data_description.max_car_power: Maximum power limit for car charging (W)
data_description.max_grid_power: Maximum power limit from grid — safety limit (W)
data_description.arbitrage_mode_max_export_power: Maximum export power used during arbitrage mode. Set to 0 to use the automatic cap from the battery and grid limits (W)
data_description.min_car_charging_threshold: Minimum power to consider car as actively charging (W)
data_description.predictive_charging_min_soc: Minimum SOC for predictive charging logic (%)
data_description.base_grid_setpoint: Base minimum grid setpoint when no monthly peak data available (W)
